final_artifact:
GABI DOU Frontend Roadmap — Prioritized Verdict

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
PHASE 1 — Implement First (maximum product impact)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. Operational Dashboard + App Shell
   Replace the hero/landing page with a dense research cockpit. Persistent top-level navigation across all routes (currently standalone). Desktop-first layout with responsive mobile fallback — DOU research is a desktop-primary, multi-tab workflow; optimizing for mobile touch targets at the expense of information density is a strategic mistake.
   Key deliverables:
   - Shared app shell with sidebar/topbar navigation, route-aware breadcrumbs.
   - Dashboard home: recent documents queue, saved searches, reading progress summary, DOU edition status.
   - Research session continuity: localStorage-backed history with one-click resume.
   Dependency: None. This is the scaffold everything else mounts into.

2. Assisted Search with DOU-Specific Facets
   "Assisted" must mean something concrete, not a vague UX aspiration. For gazette research, the minimum viable assistance is structured filtering over the DOU's own taxonomy.
   Key deliverables:
   - Faceted query builder: date-range picker, DOU section filters (Seção 1/2/3,Extra), act-type classification (Portaria, Edital, Decreto, etc.).
   - Persistent query history per session.
   - Result snippets with keyword hit-highlighting and section context — DOU documents are too long for title-only result lists.
   - Autocomplete seeded from recent queries and known entity names.
   Dependency: App shell (search lives in the shell header and as a dashboard module).

3. High-Performance Document Reader
   Long DOU editions can contain thousands of acts. Rendering them monolithically will crash mobile browsers and lag desktop. Performance is not optional; it is the feature gate.
   Key deliverables:
   - Virtualized scrolling (react-window or equivalent) with section-based lazy loading as a hard prerequisite.
   - TOC sidebar generated from document section headings, with scroll-spy active state.
   - URL hash deep-linking to specific sections (enables citation sharing).
   - localStorage scroll-position restoration (resume reading where you left off).
   - Existing reading-progress hooks wired into visible UI (progress bar, TOC position indicator).
   Dependency: App shell (reader is a route within the shell).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
EXPLICITLY DEFERRED
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

- Motion tokens / animation polish — Decorative. Tokens exist, application can wait until Phase 1 surfaces are stable. Zero research workflow impact.
- PDF export / share-at-position — Browser print-to-PDF covers 80% of the need. Deep-link sharing (Phase 1, item 3) solves the citation use case more cleanly. Revisit only if user research shows demand.
- Concurso tabular text parser — High potential value but narrow audience segment. Requires its own data-modeling work (structured table extraction) that doesn't block the core research loop. Candidate for Phase 2 once the reader infrastructure exists to display parsed tables.
- Mobile-first optimization — Explicitly deprioritized. The app must be responsive and usable on mobile, but layout decisions should optimize for desktop information density. Mobile-specific UX (swipe gestures, bottom sheets) is Phase 3 at earliest.
- Multi-document comparison (split-view / tabbed editions) — Valid research need (retificações, cross-edition comparison) but architecturally complex. Requires the reader and app shell to be stable first. Logged as a Phase 2 requirement; not in scope for Phase 1.
- Safe image fallback system — Already working. Keep as-is. No investment needed.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
KNOWN LIMITATIONS (documented, not ignored)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

- No cross-document comparison workflow until Phase 2. Researchers needing to compare retificações must use browser tabs.
- No structured concurso data extraction. Tabular plain-text content renders as-is in the reader without semantic parsing.
- Mobile experience is functional but not optimized. Complex faceted search and long-document reading will be compromised on small screens.

-Frontend target for GABI DOU:
-- Replace the current landing-like home with a true operational dashboard.
-- Add an app shell with persistent navigation, mobile-first but strong on desktop.
-- Improve search to feel assisted, not just a plain input.
-- Upgrade the document reader for very long DOU documents with section-aware reading UX.
-- Keep the existing safe image fallback system.
-- Prioritize functionality and research workflow over decorative polish.
+GABI DOU Frontend Roadmap — Prioritized Verdict
 
-Current concrete gaps observed in code:
-- No shared app shell; routes are standalone.
-- Home still has centered hero/title/copy and lacks recent history, richer status, and actionable density.
-- Document page does not integrate reading progress, TOC, deep-linking, or saved reading position, despite hooks existing.
-- No PDF/share-at-position flow.
-- No parser for tabular concurso-style plain text.
-- Motion tokens exist but are barely applied.

+   Dependency: App shell (reader is a route within the shell).
+
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
+EXPLICITLY DEFERRED
+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+- Motion tokens / animation polish — Decorative. Tokens exist, application can wait until Phase 1 surfaces are stable. Zero research workflow impact.
+- PDF export / share-at-position — Browser print-to-PDF covers 80% of the need. Deep-link sharing (Phase 1, item 3) solves the citation use case more cleanly. Revisit only if user research shows demand.
+- Concurso tabular text parser — High potential value but narrow audience segment. Requires its own data-modeling work (structured table extraction) that doesn't block the core research loop. Candidate for Phase 2 once the reader infrastructure exists to display parsed tables.
+- Mobile-first optimization — Explicitly deprioritized. The app must be responsive and usable on mobile, but layout decisions should optimize for desktop information density. Mobile-specific UX (swipe gestures, bottom sheets) is Phase 3 at earliest.
+- Multi-document comparison (split-view / tabbed editions) — Valid research need (retificações, cross-edition comparison) but architecturally complex. Requires the reader and app shell to be stable first. Logged as a Phase 2 requirement; not in scope for Phase 1.
+- Safe image fallback system — Already working. Keep as-is. No investment needed.
+

+═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

objections.description: The roadmap presents a disorganized feature laundry list rather than a workflow-centric execution plan. It lists six parallel workstreams without sequencing for research utility, forcing engineering to guess at dependencies.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly sequence three phases: (1) Persistent App Shell with Research Session history/queue, (2) Assisted Search with DOU-specific facets, (3) Virtualized Document Reader with deep-linking. Defer all polish items.

objections.description: Assisted search is hand-wavy vaporware. No specification of the assistance mechanism (autocomplete? boolean syntax? entity extraction?) or DOU-specific affordances like date-range filtering or section facets, which are critical for gazette research.
objections.suggested_fix: Define assisted search as: faceted query builder with date range picker, DOU section filters (Admin, Judiciário, etc.), and persistent query history with result highlighting.

objections.description: Claims to support very long DOU documents but gaps analysis omits virtualization or incremental loading strategies. Rendering a 10,000-item Diário Oficial in a browser without virtualization will crash mobile and lag desktop.
objections.suggested_fix: Mandate virtualized scrolling (react-window or similar) and section-based lazy loading as non-negotiable prerequisites to TOC and reading position features.

objections.description: Missing multi-document comparison workflow. DOU research is fundamentally cross-referential (comparing editions, checking retificações), yet the roadmap only supports single-document reading.
objections.suggested_fix: Add Phase 2 requirement for tabbed document interface or split-view comparison mode; if out of scope, explicitly document this limitation.

suggestions.description: PRIORITY 1: Operational Dashboard App Shell. Replace hero with dense research cockpit: recent documents queue, saved search alerts, and reading progress summary. This maximizes researcher efficiency immediately.
PRIORITY 2: Assisted Search with Context. Implement faceted filters (date, section, act type) and result snippets with hit-highlighting. DOU documents are too long for blind navigation.
PRIORITY 3: High-Performance Document Reader. Virtualized rendering + TOC sidebar + URL hash deep-linking + localStorage scroll position restoration. Enables reliable citation and resumption.

DEFERRED: Motion tokens (decorative), PDF/share-at-position (browsers handle this), concurso parser (narrow use case), and mobile-first emphasis (DOU research is desktop-primary; mobile is secondary).

objections.description: User task explicitly requested a prioritized verdict (3 areas first, what deferred). Artifact lists goals without ranking or deferral list, failing the core prompt requirement.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit 'Phase 1' and 'Deferred' sections to the roadmap to satisfy the prioritization constraint.

objections.description: 'Mobile-first' is a strategic risk for DOU research tools which are desktop-heavy (legal/comparison workflows). Prioritizing mobile may compromise power-user density.
objections.suggested_fix: Change strategy to 'Desktop-first with mobile responsiveness' to match actual user workflow.

objections.description: Parser for tabular concurso-style text is listed as a gap but missing from the 'Frontend target' list despite high product impact for DOU.
objections.suggested_fix: Move parser implementation into Phase 1 targets or explicitly defer with justification.

suggestions.description: Implement App Shell, Assisted Search, and Document Reader as Phase 1. Defer Motion tokens and Mobile optimization.
Explicitly add 'Concurso Text Parser' to high-priority functionality list.

- - Prioritize functionality and research workflow over decorative polish.

orchestrator_response.content:
GABI DOU Frontend Roadmap — Prioritized Verdict

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
PHASE 1 — Implement First (maximum product impact)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. Operational Dashboard + App Shell
   Replace the hero/landing page with a dense research cockpit. Persistent top-level navigation across all routes (currently standalone). Desktop-first layout with responsive mobile fallback — DOU research is a desktop-primary, multi-tab workflow; optimizing for mobile touch targets at the expense of information density is a strategic mistake.
   Key deliverables:
   - Shared app shell with sidebar/topbar navigation, route-aware breadcrumbs.
   - Dashboard home: recent documents queue, saved searches, reading progress summary, DOU edition status.
   - Research session continuity: localStorage-backed history with one-click resume.
   Dependency: None. This is the scaffold everything else mounts into.

2. Assisted Search with DOU-Specific Facets
   "Assisted" must mean something concrete, not a vague UX aspiration. For gazette research, the minimum viable assistance is structured filtering over the DOU's own taxonomy.
   Key deliverables:
   - Faceted query builder: date-range picker, DOU section filters (Seção 1/2/3,Extra), act-type classification (Portaria, Edital, Decreto, etc.).
   - Persistent query history per session.
   - Result snippets with keyword hit-highlighting and section context — DOU documents are too long for title-only result lists.
   - Autocomplete seeded from recent queries and known entity names.
   Dependency: App shell (search lives in the shell header and as a dashboard module).

3. High-Performance Document Reader
   Long DOU editions can contain thousands of acts. Rendering them monolithically will crash mobile browsers and lag desktop. Performance is not optional; it is the feature gate.
   Key deliverables:
   - Virtualized scrolling (react-window or equivalent) with section-based lazy loading as a hard prerequisite.
   - TOC sidebar generated from document section headings, with scroll-spy active state.
   - URL hash deep-linking to specific sections (enables citation sharing).
   - localStorage scroll-position restoration (resume reading where you left off).
   - Existing reading-progress hooks wired into visible UI (progress bar, TOC position indicator).
   Dependency: App shell (reader is a route within the shell).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
EXPLICITLY DEFERRED
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

- Motion tokens / animation polish — Decorative. Tokens exist, application can wait until Phase 1 surfaces are stable. Zero research workflow impact.
- PDF export / share-at-position — Browser print-to-PDF covers 80% of the need. Deep-link sharing (Phase 1, item 3) solves the citation use case more cleanly. Revisit only if user research shows demand.
- Concurso tabular text parser — High potential value but narrow audience segment. Requires its own data-modeling work (structured table extraction) that doesn't block the core research loop. Candidate for Phase 2 once the reader infrastructure exists to display parsed tables.
- Mobile-first optimization — Explicitly deprioritized. The app must be responsive and usable on mobile, but layout decisions should optimize for desktop information density. Mobile-specific UX (swipe gestures, bottom sheets) is Phase 3 at earliest.
- Multi-document comparison (split-view / tabbed editions) — Valid research need (retificações, cross-edition comparison) but architecturally complex. Requires the reader and app shell to be stable first. Logged as a Phase 2 requirement; not in scope for Phase 1.
- Safe image fallback system — Already working. Keep as-is. No investment needed.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
KNOWN LIMITATIONS (documented, not ignored)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

- No cross-document comparison workflow until Phase 2. Researchers needing to compare retificações must use browser tabs.
- No structured concurso data extraction. Tabular plain-text content renders as-is in the reader without semantic parsing.
- Mobile experience is functional but not optimized. Complex faceted search and long-document reading will be compromised on small screens.
